game_name: Police Quest: In Pursuit of the Death Angel

design_philosophy.core_loop: Follow proper police procedures to investigate crime scenes, arrest suspects, and gather evidence while maintaining strict adherence to protocol and duty regulations
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Procedural correctness vs. player intuition; failure to follow exact police protocol results in mission failure or death
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Authentic police detective work—solving crimes through proper investigative procedure rather than puzzle-solving or action heroics
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Prioritizes realism and procedural authenticity over traditional adventure game puzzles; game is fundamentally about *how* you do things (proper order of actions, correct equipment handling) rather than *what* you do. Designed as a police simulator with a technical advisor (actual CHP officer Jim Walls) ensuring accuracy. Lacks 'traditional' puzzles entirely—stands apart from contemporary Sierra titles like Leisure Suit Larry and King's Quest through its grounded, serious tone.

difficulty.lives_system: Single life; failure to follow procedure or death results in game over
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint-based progression through scenarios; must restart scenario on failure
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Low agency in difficulty adjustment. Game enforces strict procedural requirements with no difficulty modes. Player must memorize and execute correct action sequences (e.g., draw weapon before firing, store sidearm at jail) in proper order. Penalties include point deductions or instant death.

progression.upgrade_system: Character promotion from patrol officer to detective as plot progresses; advancement unlocks new investigative tools and partnerships
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-driven; completing assigned tasks (securing crime scenes, recovering vehicles, arrests) triggers promotion to Narcotics Division and detective partnership with Laura Watts
replayability_hooks: Multiple procedural paths through investigations (though most lead to failure if incorrect)
Point-based performance tracking based on procedural adherence
1992 SCI remake with updated graphics and point-and-click interface provides mechanically different experience from original AGI text-based version

world_building_techniques: Manual documentation of authentic police procedures required for gameplay
Environmental interaction tied to narrative (crime scenes, suspect locations, undercover gambling operation)
NPC dialogue revealing character backstory (Jack Cobb's daughter's drug addiction, Marie Wilkans as former high school crush)
Protagonist motivation through emotional stakes (Kathy Cobb's overdose death drives final confrontation)
Setting detail (Lytton, California; Hotel Delphoria; The Blue Room café)
Plot progression through logical investigative sequence: patrol → crime discovery → promotion → undercover operation → climax

boss_design.philosophy: Single antagonist (Jesse Bains, 'The Death Angel') represents culmination of investigation rather than combat encounter. Boss confrontation is narrative climax triggered by undercover infiltration, not skill-based fight. Resolution occurs through backup team arrival rather than player direct action.
notable_bosses: Jesse Bains ('The Death Angel') - drug lord running gambling operation at Hotel Delphoria; final confrontation occurs in penthouse after Bonds reveals identity; arrested after backup team intervention

faction_indicators: Lytton Police Department hierarchy (Sergeant Dooley as supervisor, transition to Narcotics Division)
Criminal underworld (drug cartel led by Bains, street-level dealers like Donald Colby)
Off-duty police culture (The Blue Room café as social hub)
Underworld intelligence network (Marie Wilkans as prostitute with information on drug ring)
danger_signals: Stolen vehicles used for drug trafficking
Jason Taselli on FBI Most Wanted List
Organized gambling operation at Hotel Delphoria
Officer vulnerability to suspect weapons (sidearm theft from unlocked locker results in death)
Character death from overdose (Kathy Cobb) signals stakes of organized drug trade
environmental_storytelling: Crime scene evidence (gunshot wound, murder weapon, drugs in vehicle trunk)
Vehicle repaint indicates criminal operation sophistication
Penthouse suite setting suggests wealth and power of final antagonist
Jail procedures gameplay reveals systemic vulnerabilities to criminal manipulation
Undercover card game infiltration demonstrates criminal hierarchy and recruitment
